{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "planned",
  "component_conditions": [],
  "percent_complete": 0,
  "value": 92,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Gusts of 28 mph exceed the manufacturer's 20 mph wind limit for this boom configuration",
    "Lift is planned at 92% of chart capacity, leaving minimal margin for dynamic wind loads"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Delay lift until wind conditions remain steady below 20 mph, including gusts",
    "Re-plan lift with a larger crane to reduce percent of chart capacity or revise schedule to mitigate standby costs",
    "Conduct an engineered lift review if wind limits cannot be reliably maintained"
  ],
  "rationale": "The gusts to 28 mph clearly exceed the manufacturer's 20 mph limit, making the lift non-compliant and unsafe regardless of schedule pressure. The high capacity utilization (92%) further reduces margin to absorb wind-induced side loads. OSHA 1926.1400 requires that crane operations stay within manufacturer limits and environmental conditions be continuously monitored; schedule urgency does not override safety limits.",
  "references": ["OSHA 1926.1400", "Manufacturer's load chart and wind limit specification"]
}
